title: write_to_radio returns borrowed reference without enforcing clear_write_buffer
description:
File: rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/gatt.rs:211-282

The write_to_radio function returns a borrowed reference to the internal buffer when a complete message is received:

```rust
pub fn write_to_radio(&mut self, chunk: &[u8]) -> Result<Option<&[u8]>, GattError> {
    ...
    return Ok(Some(&self.write_buffer[..expected as usize]));
}
```

The API contract requires callers to call clear_write_buffer() after processing the returned data, but this is not enforced by the type system. If a caller forgets:

1. The next write_to_radio call will corrupt/append to the previous message's buffer
2. The state machine (write_expected_len) will be in an inconsistent state
3. Subsequent messages will be malformed or incorrectly parsed

IMPACT: Hard-to-debug corruption of BLE message parsing.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. Return owned data (Vec) instead of borrowed slice to auto-clear
2. Use a builder pattern that consumes self
3. Add debug assertions to detect misuse
4. Document the contract more prominently with SAFETY comments
close_reason: Added auto-clear of previous state in write_to_radio to prevent buffer corruption if clear_write_buffer is not called. Updated to match Python implementation behavior. Tests pass.
